sources.writer_profile: Alfonso Campos, writing as El Gocho Gourmet, is a Granada-born food critic and hospitality professional who trained at the city's Escuela de Hostelería Hurtado de Mendoza and has worked in several of Granada's most respected restaurants, including Carmen de Aben Humeya and Restaurante Arriaga. He is the creator of the El Gocho Gourmet restaurant guide to Granada — now in its second edition with Ayuntamiento backing — and writes a regular gastronomy column for GranadaDigital, Granada's leading independent digital newspaper. His editorial focus centres on authentic, locally rooted establishments rather than tourist-facing venues. NOTE: The El Gocho Gourmet column launched in April 2025; verify depth of Granada coverage in future runs.

James Dimitri is an independent food and travel writer who visited Granada in February 2025 and documented the city's distinctive free-tapa bar culture with first-hand recommendations. His writing focuses on the authentic everyday dining experience — in particular, the tapas bar tradition that distinguishes Granada from other Spanish cities. His guide reflects the perspective of a knowledgeable, independently minded visitor looking for genuine local bars rather than tourist-facing restaurants.

Lauren Aloise is an American food writer who studied in Granada and has returned to the city more than twenty times, building deep personal knowledge of its dining scene. She writes the Spanish Sabores food blog and is also co-founder of Devour Tours, a commercial food tour operator active in several Spanish cities. ⚠ coi: her commercial food tour interests should be noted when weighing her restaurant recommendations.
sources.commercial_conflict_note: Lauren Aloise is co-founder of Devour Tours, a commercial food tour operator active in Spain. ⚠ coi

sources.writer_profile: Roberto Ruiz writes for eldiario.es, a national Spanish digital newspaper with strong independent editorial standards. His 2023 article on Granada's essential tapas bars is a focused survey of long-standing Granada classics written from a local journalism perspective, with Spanish-language descriptions for a national Spanish audience.

restaurants.name: Bar Los Diamantes
restaurants.signature_dish: Pescadito frito; gambas a la plancha; champiñones salteados
restaurants.quote: Open since 1942, specialising in pescadito frito, gambas a la plancha, and champiñones salteados. Cram in for a caña at the Calle Navas or Plaza Nueva location and receive a generous free tapa — Granada's benchmark seafood tapas bar.

restaurants.name: Bodegas Castañeda
restaurants.quote: Established in 1936 with fortified wine barrels behind the counter — 'uno de los bares con más solera y tradición', a step from Gran Vía (eldiario, 2023). Go in at aperitif time and you will understand why regulars call it an old-man bar in the best sense.

restaurants.name: Café 4 Gatos
restaurants.signature_dish: Toast with tomato; manchego and jamón toast
restaurants.quote: Breakfast café in the Albaicín with enormous toasts — with tomato, manchego, or jamón — good coffee and fresh orange juice; options well beyond the standard tostada at a distinctly local spot.

restaurants.name: Tajine Elvira
restaurants.quote: My favorite restaurant for authentic Moroccan food in Granada — started as a tiny hole-in-the-wall run by a husband-and-wife team, now in a slightly larger space on Calle Elvira.
